text.title: Django Testing with pytest Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Tests use TestCase subclasses; pytest fixtures don't compose without convoluted super() workarounds.
antipattern.root-cause: LLM defaulted to unittest because it's the historical default.
antipattern.fix: Convert to def test_* functions with @pytest.mark.django_db. Move setUp logic into pytest fixtures.

antipattern.symptom: Adding a non-nullable field to User breaks 40 tests with 'IntegrityError: null value in column'.
antipattern.root-cause: Conftest fixtures call User.objects.create_user with hard-coded fields.
antipattern.fix: Replace fixtures with factory_boy factories that define sensible defaults; tests only override fields they care about.

antipattern.symptom: 10 nearly-identical test_create_N functions; failure log shows test_create_7 failed with no description.
antipattern.root-cause: Author duplicated tests for input variants instead of using parametrize.
Author over-mocked instead of using a thin integration test.
antipattern.fix: Mock only direct collaborator; for deeper dependencies, use a real DB + responses for HTTP.

antipattern.symptom: Failing test passes after the agent made an undocumented production edit; bug ships behind the green test.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent treated the failing test as the problem; edited prod code without surfacing the diff.
antipattern.fix: Wrap LLM TDD flow with a human-in-loop checkpoint that requires the diff to be shown before edits to non-test files.
